item.title: July CNA Session - 07/16/2026 12:00am UTC to 07/16/2026 11:59pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Becoming a Nurse Aide may be for you! Nurse Aides provide a variety of care-related tasks such as dressing, feeding and observing patients. Nurse Aides usually work in long-term care facilities, home health agencies, assisted living facilities and hospitals. After completing this course, students will be prepared to take the State written and competency exam. State exam fees are not included in the cost of this class. State exams are completed through D & S Diversified Technologies. Clinical locations will be announced in class. Before the first day of class, students must read and sign a code of conduct contract. A Terra State Kern Center representative will go over this contract with you when you register. All classes will take place at the Fostoria Learning Center. 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830 CONTACT(419) 559-2464kern@terra.edu 75 hours total 59 hours in class time 16 hours of Clinicals Cost: $950/student, first state test fee included. Max Class Size: 8 Before Clinicals each student will need following: TB Test Physical *Scholarships available to eligible learners. For scholarship information, contact via email or phone: eriehle01@terra.edu / 419-559-2255
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: July CNA Session on Jul 16, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. This is an all-day event with themes including scholarships available and Health & Fitness.

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/16/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/16/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 47 Clay St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Simply Shop Local
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 16, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include interactive experience and local businesses.

item.title: Dino Tea Party - 07/16/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/16/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a "Dino Tea Party" on Thursday, July 16 at 2:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room at Tiffin-Seneca Public Library! Designed for preschoolers through second graders, this whimsical event will feature a mix of prehistoric fun and traditional tea party delights. Attendees will enjoy storytelling, light refreshments, and dino-themed crafts, all while dressed as their favorite dinosaurs or in their fanciest tea attire. Space is limited, so please register by calling (419) 447-3751 or visiting the Youth Services desk starting July 1. This event is part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program, “Unearth a Story.” For more information, visit http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Dino Tea Party on Jul 16, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include tiffin, ohio and Seasonal & Holiday.

item.description:
Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Music & Lyrics by Karey Kirkpatrick & Wayne Kirkpatrick Book by Karey Kirkpatrick & John O’Farrell July 16, 17, 24, 2027 – 7:30 PM July 25, 2027 – 2:00 PM A Ritz Players Production Almost, Maine is a popular play by John Cariani, a romantic comedy composed of nine interconnected short plays set in the mythical, remote town of Almost, Maine, on a cold winter night. item.title: "Man Made" Art Show, Displayed July 1st-18th - 07/16/2026 9:00pm EDT to 07/16/2026 10:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

AG’S MAN MADE ART SHOW The Tiffin Art Guild proudly presents the Man Made Art Show, a special exhibit celebrating the creativity, skill, and artistic vision of male artists. Created as a companion exhibit to our popular She Speaks Art Show, this exhibition provides an opportunity for men to share their unique perspectives through painting, drawing, sculpture, photography, woodworking, mixed media, and more. Whether your work is bold and powerful, thoughtful and reflective, or somewhere in between, we invite you to be part of this exciting showcase. Join us as we fill the gallery with original works that inspire conversation, celebrate creativity, and demonstrate that art knows no boundaries. We look forward to showcasing the many ways men create, imagine, and leave their mark through art. Rob Ledwedge President We are grateful to Seneca Caverns for sponsoring!!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild
item.yodel:media_alt_text: "Man Made" Art Show, Displayed July 1st-18th on Jul 16, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include tiffin art guild and tiffin, ohio.

item.title: LIFEGUARDING COURSE - 07/17/2026 12:00am EDT to 07/19/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 180 Summit St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Ready to make a difference this summer? 🌊☀️

Become a certified lifeguard and gain the skills to save lives, build confidence, and become a leader!

Important Details:

JULY 17-19, 2026

Friday 4p-8p, Saturday 8a-5p, & Sunday 8a-5p

• Blended course format – 7 hours of online coursework must be completed before the first day of class

• Swim assessment required one week prior to the first class- Friday at 6pm

• Attendance is mandatory for all scheduled days (no make-up sessions)

• Registration closes one week before the class begins

• Limited to just 8 students per class – reserve your spot early!

Don’t wait—spots fill fast! 📅 Enroll now at the Tiffin Community YMCA and dive into a new opportunity!
item.dc:creator: YMCA of Bucyrus-Tiffin, Inc
item.yodel:media_alt_text: LIFEGUARDING COURSE on Jul 17, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. This is an all-day event with themes including lifeguarding and summer.

item.title: July CNA Session - 07/18/2026 12:00am UTC to 07/18/2026 11:59pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Becoming a Nurse Aide may be for you! Nurse Aides provide a variety of care-related tasks such as dressing, feeding and observing patients. Nurse Aides usually work in long-term care facilities, home health agencies, assisted living facilities and hospitals. After completing this course, students will be prepared to take the State written and competency exam. State exam fees are not included in the cost of this class. State exams are completed through D & S Diversified Technologies. Clinical locations will be announced in class. Before the first day of class, students must read and sign a code of conduct contract. A Terra State Kern Center representative will go over this contract with you when you register. All classes will take place at the Fostoria Learning Center. 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830 CONTACT(419) 559-2464kern@terra.edu 75 hours total 59 hours in class time 16 hours of Clinicals Cost: $950/student, first state test fee included. Max Class Size: 8 Before Clinicals each student will need following: TB Test Physical *Scholarships available to eligible learners. For scholarship information, contact via email or phone: eriehle01@terra.edu / 419-559-2255

item.title: Free Community Health Fair - 07/18/2026 9:00am EDT to 07/18/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 500 E Jackson St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA

Showing Love to Our Neighbors

Learn how to take charge of your health and overall well-being by attending our free Community Health and Wellness Fair July 18, 2026. Healthcare professionals and members of local organizations will be present to help you start your wellness journey. Come burn some energy in the Youth Fitness Zone, get your face painted or allow the caricature artist to capture your best side. A local food truck willl be available for event attendees to purchase a bite to eat. This family friendly event will be held at Bethel Baptist Church, 500 E. Jackson St. Fostoria, Ohio 44830. The hours will be 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM.

We look forward to helping Fostoria become a healthier and happier community!

item.title: Walk at the Rock - 07/18/2026 9:00am EDT to 07/18/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 7353 County Rd 29, Flat Rock, OH 44828, USA

We invite families, friends, churches, partners, community groups, and supporters to tie on their walking shoes and join us on Saturday, July 18, for a morning of fun, fellowship, and fundraising.

Registration begins at 9:00 AM, followed by the residents’ Walk of Hope at 10:00 AM and the Walk at the Rock 1K shortly after. Participation is free, with the opportunity to support our fundraiser through the purchase of event T-shirts, residents’ handmade items, and concessions.

We will recognize the top fundraising church and top fundraising team! Rally your family, friends, supporters, and parishioners to help make a difference and take home one of this year’s fundraising awards!

In addition to the walk, this year’s event will feature several new family-friendly activities, including a dunk tank, water features, inflatable axe-throwing, coffee, dirty soda and treats, a resident’s art exhibit, and more.
item.dc:creator: Flat Rock Homes
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Walk at the Rock on Jul 18, 2026 in Flat Rock, OH. Featured themes include Health & Fitness and Food & Drink.

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/18/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/18/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Shop Tiffin Ohio
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 18, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Seasonal & Holiday and interactive experience.

item.title: Lantern Tour - 07/18/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/18/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 15248 E Township Rd 178, Bellevue, OH 44811, USA Lantern Tour: Explore the Depths by Lantern Light! Step back in time and descend into the shadows on our captivating one-hour Lantern Tour! Relive the thrilling experience of early explorers as you venture 65 feet below the surface of Seneca Caverns. Guided by lantern light and your own headlamp, you’ll navigate rugged passageways and uncover the eerie ghost stories, local folklore, and fascinating history of this Ohio landmark. A Lantern Tour is the perfect way to step into adventure! What to Expect: A one-hour guided trek through the unique underground environment. Exploration by the atmospheric glow of lanterns and headlamps. Navigating natural stone steps and pathways, with some low-ceiling areas. Engaging tales of ghost stories, local legends, and the rich history of Seneca Caverns. Important Notes: Age Requirement: Suitable for ages 12 and older only due to the nature of the tour. What to Bring: Your own headlamp (optional, we will have some available if needed). What to Wear: A jacket or sweatshirt (cave temperature is 54°F) and closed-toe shoes (required). Booking: Advance registration is required. Ticket sales close 24 hours before the scheduled tour time. Group Size: Limited to 10 participants per tour. Spaces are limited, so book your adventure today! Price: $32 per person (ages 12 and older) Book Online Now
item.dc:creator: Seneca Caverns
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Lantern Tour on Jul 18, 2026 in Bellevue, OH. Featured themes include bellevue, ohio and ghost stories.

item.title: JAM’s Growing together Kids Class series July Lizard Lessons and Creative Adventures - 07/19/2026 12:00pm EDT to 07/19/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for Lizard Lessons & Creative Adventures, a fun-filled event inspired by the book Who Pooped On Me? by David Cunliffe! Kids will explore the wonders of nature, curiosity, and creativity through exciting hands-on activities and colorful crafts. Young explorers will decorate their own wooden lizard with bright paints and fun designs, then create a sweet and cheerful ice cream craft that shows off their imagination and personality. Along the way, kids will discover an important lesson from the story, it’s okay not to have all the answers! Asking questions, exploring nature, and trying new things help us learn, grow, and become confident creators. This playful event encourages curiosity, kindness, creativity, and self-expression in a positive and welcoming environment where every child can shine. Explore nature. Create boldly. Learn through curiosity. Have fun along the way! Ages 9 and up Location: JAM’s Markets Date & Time: Sunday July 19th 12:00 pm
item.dc:creator: JAM's Monthly Markets
item.yodel:media_alt_text: JAM’s Growing together Kids Class series July Lizard Lessons and Creative Adventures on Jul 19, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include creative adventures and jam’s growing together kids class series july lizard lessons and creative adventures.

Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Steinmetz Signs & Graphic Ink (SSG Ink - Shortened)
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 19, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include interactive experience and seasonal.

item.title: July CNA Session - 07/20/2026 12:00am UTC to 07/20/2026 11:59pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Becoming a Nurse Aide may be for you! Nurse Aides provide a variety of care-related tasks such as dressing, feeding and observing patients. Nurse Aides usually work in long-term care facilities, home health agencies, assisted living facilities and hospitals. After completing this course, students will be prepared to take the State written and competency exam. State exam fees are not included in the cost of this class. State exams are completed through D & S Diversified Technologies. Clinical locations will be announced in class. Before the first day of class, students must read and sign a code of conduct contract. A Terra State Kern Center representative will go over this contract with you when you register. All classes will take place at the Fostoria Learning Center. 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830 CONTACT(419) 559-2464kern@terra.edu 75 hours total 59 hours in class time 16 hours of Clinicals Cost: $950/student, first state test fee included. Max Class Size: 8 Before Clinicals each student will need following: TB Test Physical *Scholarships available to eligible learners. For scholarship information, contact via email or phone: eriehle01@terra.edu / 419-559-2255
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: July CNA Session on Jul 20, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. This is an all-day event with themes including long term care facilities and nurse aide.

item.title: Flower Hammering at Kaubisch Memorial Public Libray - 07/20/2026 12:00am EDT to 07/20/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 205 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Join us for a creative workshop, Flower Hammering/Pounding, at the Kaubisch Memorial Public Library led by Barn Yard Posies. Participants will use rubber mallets to transfer vibrant blooms onto a 28"x28" white tea cloth. Flowers will be provided, but feel free to bring your own favorites like geraniums, petunias, sunpatiens, and marigolds. The cost is $15, and registration and payment are required by July 17. For questions, contact the library at 419-435-2813, ext. 123. Don't miss this chance to create beautiful floral art!
item.dc:creator: Barn Yard Posies
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Flower Hammering at Kaubisch Memorial Public Libray on Jul 20, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include Classes/Workshops and Arts.

item.title: Cold Case Camp - 07/20/2026 8:30am EDT to 07/23/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 155 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA If Law and Order, CSI and NCIS are on regular rotation and science has become a guilty pleasure, it is time to take it to the next level with Tiffin University Cold Case Camp. TU’s Cold Case Camp is an immersive camp that will acquaint high school students with cold case work and introduce them to the many concepts involved in the solving of cold cases. Students will be exposed to both mock and real cold cases in a hands-on capacity. The camp will be led by TU School of Criminal Justice and Social Sciences faculty and professionals experienced in conducting criminal investigations at all levels.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin University
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Cold Case Camp on Jul 20, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include tiffin, ohio and History & Museums.

item.title: Motors and Motor Controls - 07/21/2026 8:00am UTC to 07/21/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length: 24 Hours $2000 per student This course is an application of common electrical concepts and relay ladder logic/motor control circuits used in industry to maintain and troubleshoot electrical power and control systems, including variable frequency drive systems. Students will learn how to install, operate, maintain, and troubleshoot single[1]phase transformers, NEMA and IEC contactors & motor starters, motor branch circuits, and reversing circuits (power & control). A heavy focus of the course will be on variable frequency drives. Students will learn to install, maintain, program and troubleshoot industrial programmable VFDs. The course will focus on 3 types of VFD: Automation Direct’s GS2, Allen Bradley PowerFlex 70 and Allen Bradley PowerFlex 525. The topics presented will be learned through online instructional material, and hands-on labs.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Motors and Motor Controls on Jul 21, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include industrial and variable frequency drives.

item.title: Summer Microsoft Excel 3 - 07/21/2026 8:00am UTC to 07/21/2026 5:00pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA $179 per person Excel is Microsoft’s powerful and easy-to-use spreadsheet program. This third part of our Microsoft 365 Excel courseware is intended to help all users get up to speed quickly on the advanced features of Excel. item.title: 6p | Trivia Tuesday: America 250 Edition! - 07/21/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/21/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for "Trivia Tuesday: America 250 Edition!" at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Tuesday, July 21, from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This adult trivia night features six rounds of exciting questions celebrating America 250, promising an evening filled with fun, facts, and friendly competition. Compete for a chance to win a custom 3D-printed trophy made at the Library. This free event is open to adults and teens, but space is limited, so registration is required. Visit our online calendar at http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/ or call (419) 447-3751 for more information.

item.title: July CNA Session - 07/22/2026 12:00am UTC to 07/22/2026 11:59pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Becoming a Nurse Aide may be for you! Nurse Aides provide a variety of care-related tasks such as dressing, feeding and observing patients. Nurse Aides usually work in long-term care facilities, home health agencies, assisted living facilities and hospitals. After completing this course, students will be prepared to take the State written and competency exam. State exam fees are not included in the cost of this class. State exams are completed through D & S Diversified Technologies. Clinical locations will be announced in class. Before the first day of class, students must read and sign a code of conduct contract. A Terra State Kern Center representative will go over this contract with you when you register. All classes will take place at the Fostoria Learning Center. 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830 CONTACT(419) 559-2464kern@terra.edu 75 hours total 59 hours in class time 16 hours of Clinicals Cost: $950/student, first state test fee included. Max Class Size: 8 Before Clinicals each student will need following: TB Test Physical *Scholarships available to eligible learners. For scholarship information, contact via email or phone: eriehle01@terra.edu / 419-559-2255
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: July CNA Session on Jul 22, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. This is an all-day event with themes including long term care and nurse aide.

item.title: Motors and Motor Controls - 07/22/2026 8:00am UTC to 07/22/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length: 24 Hours $2000 per student This course is an application of common electrical concepts and relay ladder logic/motor control circuits used in industry to maintain and troubleshoot electrical power and control systems, including variable frequency drive systems. Students will learn how to install, operate, maintain, and troubleshoot single[1]phase transformers, NEMA and IEC contactors & motor starters, motor branch circuits, and reversing circuits (power & control). A heavy focus of the course will be on variable frequency drives. Students will learn to install, maintain, program and troubleshoot industrial programmable VFDs. The course will focus on 3 types of VFD: Automation Direct’s GS2, Allen Bradley PowerFlex 70 and Allen Bradley PowerFlex 525. The topics presented will be learned through online instructional material, and hands-on labs.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Motors and Motor Controls on Jul 22, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include industrial and motors and motor controls.

item.title: Chamber & Development Chat and Assist | July 2026 - 07/22/2026 10:00am EDT to 07/22/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We've opened the doors to provide a better member experience for our chamber & development members and to help you get the most of your membership, ask questions, and get help to elevate your business! Chamber & Development Chat and Assist is an opportunity for our members to meet one on one with a Chamber & Development team member to understand and take advantage everything that comes with their membership, the opportunity to get help with anything from social media tips to Canva crash courses and beyond, to providing feedback to us so we can get better! Upcoming 2026 Chat & Assist dates include: Wednesday, August 26, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, September 23, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM Wednesday, October 28, 2026 from 10 AM to 1 PM You can secure a time (this is just so we know who to expect and how to best prepare!) at https://calendar.google.com/calendar/appointments/schedules/AcZssZ2Jz5jSs5OdbQ7h4VKhoLDPFhqgu-Mn_BQMaOLPCjqaRAYRVH5g0-KWk20-vt5tHmtF304_kzc6.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Chamber & Development Chat and Assist | July 2026 on Jul 22, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include member experience and social media tips.

item.title: Rubber Ducky Wednesday - 07/22/2026 11:00am EDT to 07/22/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Youth Services Department at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library will host “Rubber Ducky Wednesday” on Wednesdays, July 1, 8, 15, and 22 at 11:00 a.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Babies age birth to 35 months and their caregivers are invited to a story time on July 1, July 8, and July 15 with books, songs and lap bounces. On July 22, join the Library for a play date with toys and books for the babies to enjoy together. This program is part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program, “Unearth a Story.” For more information, stop by the Junior Library, call 419-447-3751, or log on to http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/22/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/22/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 70 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Simply Susan's
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 22, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Lifestyle and family activity.

item.title: Dinosaur Terrariums - 07/22/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/22/2026 3:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for a hands-on "Dinosaur Terrariums" workshop on Wednesday, July 22, from 2:00 to 3:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Designed for teens entering grades six through twelve, this exciting event allows participants to build their own miniature prehistoric world using layers of soil, rocks, moss, plants, and dinosaur figurines. Each teen will create and decorate a unique dinosaur habitat to take home. Registration is required and can be completed by visiting the library's online calendar at http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/. This workshop is part of the library's 2026 Summer Reading Program, "Unearth a Story." For further information, please call 419-447-3751 or visit the library website.

item.title: "Beginning Needle Felting" Wednesday, July 22, 2026 6pm-9pm #07222601 - 07/22/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/22/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Preregistration is required by Monday, July 20. In this class you will learn about needle felting surfaces, wools and fibers to use, and types of felting needles and when to use them. A finished 4” x 4” felted picture will be completed during class. All supplies are provided. . Minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Contact Beth Schweitzer (419-630-3850), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.

item.title: Teen Art Club - 07/23/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/23/2026 3:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Teen Art Club hosted by the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for an exciting opportunity to unleash your creativity! This event will take place on Thursdays, July 9 and 23, from 2:00 to 3:30 p.m. in the Junior Home Room. Open to teens in grades 6 through 12, participants are encouraged to bring their own drawings and projects, or simply drop in to explore new crafts. The library will provide various craft supplies and the chance to learn new artistic skills as part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program called “Unearth a Story.” For more details, visit http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/ or call 419-447-3751.

item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tiffin-Seneca Public Library invites families to participate in the "Roots & Branches Family Art Project" on Thursday, July 23 at 6:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This creative, hands-on program encourages families of all ages to explore and share their unique traditions through art. Attendees will create a special project inspired by their favorite customs, holidays, or everyday routines. Join us for an opportunity to make something meaningful together as part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program "Unearth a Story." For more information, call (419) 447-3751 or visit http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/.

item.title: July CNA Session - 07/24/2026 12:00am UTC to 07/24/2026 11:59pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Becoming a Nurse Aide may be for you! Nurse Aides provide a variety of care-related tasks such as dressing, feeding and observing patients. Nurse Aides usually work in long-term care facilities, home health agencies, assisted living facilities and hospitals. After completing this course, students will be prepared to take the State written and competency exam. State exam fees are not included in the cost of this class. State exams are completed through D & S Diversified Technologies. Clinical locations will be announced in class. Before the first day of class, students must read and sign a code of conduct contract. A Terra State Kern Center representative will go over this contract with you when you register. All classes will take place at the Fostoria Learning Center. 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830 CONTACT(419) 559-2464kern@terra.edu 75 hours total 59 hours in class time 16 hours of Clinicals Cost: $950/student, first state test fee included. Max Class Size: 8 Before Clinicals each student will need following: TB Test Physical *Scholarships available to eligible learners. For scholarship information, contact via email or phone: eriehle01@terra.edu / 419-559-2255

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/24/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/24/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 186 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Washington Street Outfitters
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase on Jul 24, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Seasonal & Holiday and Kids & Family.

item.title: Christmas in July - 07/24/2026 5:00pm EDT to 07/24/2026 11:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 4044 W Township Rd 98, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Get ready for a magical weekend of holiday cheer at The Barn at Seneca Hills in Tiffin! This exciting two-day event is packed with festive fun for the whole family. What to Expect: • Holiday-Themed Activities: Dive into a variety of fun crafts and activities that celebrate the spirit of Christmas. Perfect for kids and adults alike! • Delicious Food Trucks: Savor a wide selection of tasty treats from some of the most popular food trucks in the area. There's something for everyone! • Festive Cocktails: Enjoy a selection of festive cocktails to get you in the holiday spirit! • Live Entertainment: Experience live performances adding to the festive atmosphere! Talent TBA • Holiday Face Painting: Add some excitement with joyful face painting for all ages, allowing everyone to embrace the holiday spirit! • Christmas Movie Under the Stars: Wind down each night with an iconic Christmas movie screening on the lawn. Bring your blankets and enjoy a cozy evening with family and friends. Don’t miss out on this unique opportunity to celebrate the joy of the season in July! Mark your calendars and join us for a weekend filled with laughter, creativity, and holiday spirit.
item.dc:creator: The Barn at Seneca Hills
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Christmas in July on Jul 24, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include live entertainment and new riegel, ohio.

item.description: Location : 47 Clay St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Simply Shop Local

item.title: Christmas in July - 07/25/2026 5:00pm EDT to 07/25/2026 11:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 4044 W Township Rd 98, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Get ready for a magical weekend of holiday cheer at The Barn at Seneca Hills in Tiffin! This exciting two-day event is packed with festive fun for the whole family. What to Expect: • Holiday-Themed Activities: Dive into a variety of fun crafts and activities that celebrate the spirit of Christmas. Perfect for kids and adults alike! • Delicious Food Trucks: Savor a wide selection of tasty treats from some of the most popular food trucks in the area. There's something for everyone! • Festive Cocktails: Enjoy a selection of festive cocktails to get you in the holiday spirit! • Live Entertainment: Experience live performances adding to the festive atmosphere! Talent TBA • Holiday Face Painting: Add some excitement with joyful face painting for all ages, allowing everyone to embrace the holiday spirit! • Christmas Movie Under the Stars: Wind down each night with an iconic Christmas movie screening on the lawn. Bring your blankets and enjoy a cozy evening with family and friends. Don’t miss out on this unique opportunity to celebrate the joy of the season in July! Mark your calendars and join us for a weekend filled with laughter, creativity, and holiday spirit.
item.dc:creator: The Barn at Seneca Hills
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Christmas in July on Jul 25, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include seasonal and Seasonal & Holiday.

item.title: Lantern Tour - 07/25/2026 6:00pm EDT to 07/25/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 15248 E Township Rd 178, Bellevue, OH 44811, USA Lantern Tour: Explore the Depths by Lantern Light! Step back in time and descend into the shadows on our captivating one-hour Lantern Tour! Relive the thrilling experience of early explorers as you venture 65 feet below the surface of Seneca Caverns. Guided by lantern light and your own headlamp, you’ll navigate rugged passageways and uncover the eerie ghost stories, local folklore, and fascinating history of this Ohio landmark. A Lantern Tour is the perfect way to step into adventure! What to Expect: A one-hour guided trek through the unique underground environment. Exploration by the atmospheric glow of lanterns and headlamps. Navigating natural stone steps and pathways, with some low-ceiling areas. Engaging tales of ghost stories, local legends, and the rich history of Seneca Caverns. Important Notes: Age Requirement: Suitable for ages 12 and older only due to the nature of the tour. What to Bring: Your own headlamp (optional, we will have some available if needed). What to Wear: A jacket or sweatshirt (cave temperature is 54°F) and closed-toe shoes (required). Booking: Advance registration is required. Ticket sales close 24 hours before the scheduled tour time. Group Size: Limited to 10 participants per tour. Spaces are limited, so book your adventure today! Price: $32 per person (ages 12 and older) Book Online Now
item.dc:creator: Seneca Caverns
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Lantern Tour on Jul 25, 2026 in Bellevue, OH. Featured themes include lantern tour and seneca caverns.

item.title: July CNA Session - 07/26/2026 12:00am UTC to 07/26/2026 11:59pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Becoming a Nurse Aide may be for you! Nurse Aides provide a variety of care-related tasks such as dressing, feeding and observing patients. Nurse Aides usually work in long-term care facilities, home health agencies, assisted living facilities and hospitals. After completing this course, students will be prepared to take the State written and competency exam. State exam fees are not included in the cost of this class. State exams are completed through D & S Diversified Technologies. Clinical locations will be announced in class. Before the first day of class, students must read and sign a code of conduct contract. A Terra State Kern Center representative will go over this contract with you when you register. All classes will take place at the Fostoria Learning Center. 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830 CONTACT(419) 559-2464kern@terra.edu 75 hours total 59 hours in class time 16 hours of Clinicals Cost: $950/student, first state test fee included. Max Class Size: 8 Before Clinicals each student will need following: TB Test Physical *Scholarships available to eligible learners. For scholarship information, contact via email or phone: eriehle01@terra.edu / 419-559-2255
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: July CNA Session on Jul 26, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. This is an all-day event with themes including long-term care facilities and nurse aide.

item.title: Farmacy Fundamentals - 07/26/2026 4:00pm EDT to 07/26/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Wanting to become more self sufficient? Learn herbalism to help you and yours?

This class takes you from seedling to medicine! Each month, we meet to tend our medicinal garden, learn about the plants themselves, wildcraft wild medicine, harvest and process plants, and make medicine!

Join us for a monthly journey that will teach you fundamentals of growing your own Farmacy! We meet 4th Sundays, 4-6 PM starting in June (through December). Whole series $250, drop-in rate $40 if there are spots left. Class limit 20.

Scholarships are available for those taking the whole course! Please email lily@havenherbs.com if you would like to apply for a scholarship!
item.dc:creator: Haven Herbs
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Farmacy Fundamentals on Jul 26, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. This is a recurring event with themes including gardening and herbalism.

item.title: Farmacy Fundamentals - 07/26/2026 4:00pm EDT to 07/26/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Reconnect with the earth and take your health into your own hands. Farmacy Fundamentals is an immersive, in-person, seven-month journey designed to transform your relationship with the plant world. Following the natural rhythm of the seasons, this hands-on class series guides you step-by-step from plotting your first medicinal seeds to stocking your home apothecary with handcrafted remedies. Whether you have a sprawling backyard or a few sunny patio pots, you will learn the ancient art and modern science of growing, tending, harvesting, and crafting your own plant medicine. June – August: Tending the garden and materia medica! As the garden grows, we dive deep into plant profiles and the essentials of botanical care. You will learn how to identify and build relationships with your herbs while exploring the actions, phytochemistry, and energetics of each plant we planted. September – October: Harvest and processing! As the season turns, we focus on reaping what we have sown. Learn the best practices for harvesting herbs at their peak potency. We will cover techniques for washing, drying, garbling, and storing plant material to ensure it remains effective throughout the year. November – December: The home apothecary! In the final months, we transform our processed harvest into potent remedies. This period is dedicated to the craft of medicine making, where you will learn to create tinctures, salves, infused oils, and syrups to support wellness through the winter months. Investment and Registration Space is limited to 20 students to ensure an intimate, hands-on learning environment. Full Season Pre-Registration: $250 Commit to the full seasonal cycle for a discounted rate. This secures your spot in all seven classes and provides the most comprehensive experience. Link below for full series registration. If you are in need of a scholarship, there are some available, just contact Lily for details. Scholarships are available for those taking the full series. Drop-In Rate: $40 per class Available on a first-come, first-served basis as space allows. Link for single classes here (indicate which month): https://buy.stripe.com/8x29AU2asgWUcmJ2Usb7y01
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center

item.title: Tiffin's Wild Goose Chase - 07/27/2026 1:00pm EDT to 07/27/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 107 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase All Summer Long MEMORIAL DAY THRU LABOR DAY Location: The City of Tiffin Get ready for a summer full of fun with Tiffin’s Wild Goose Chase, brought to you by Simply Shop Local! Spend the summer exploring Tiffin as you search for porch geese at participating local businesses throughout the city. Each stop is a chance to discover new places, support local, and enjoy a unique, interactive experience with friends and family. How It Works: 1. Pick up a paper passport at any participating business OR 2. Get your online pass here https://www.simplyshoplocal.org/blank/summer-1 • Visit participating businesses around Tiffin • Find the porch goose at each location • Locate & record the 4 digit code for each goose. • Explore, shop, and enjoy the journey all summer long Whether you’re competing to find them all or just enjoying the adventure, the Wild Goose Chase is the perfect way to experience everything Tiffin has to offer.
item.dc:creator: Steinmetz Signs & Graphic Ink (SSG Ink - Shortened)

item.description: Location : 161 OH-101, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA We know it's not National Prayer Day anymore, but we are having a Prayer Day on July 28th. Between the hours of 9AM and 3PM you can come into the Church and pray for whatever it is that's worrying you or whatever praises you feel the need to tell Jesus. THEN we are then having a community prayer time at Hedges Boyer Park at 7:30PM! We'd love to see you all join us!

item.title: Teen Summer Reading Wrap-Up Party - 07/28/2026 2:00pm EDT to 07/28/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the Teen Summer Reading Wrap-Up Party at Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Tuesday, July 28, from 2:00 to 3:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Teens entering grades six through twelve are invited to celebrate the conclusion of the 2026 summer reading program, “Unearth a Story.” Enjoy an afternoon filled with pizza, ice cream, games, and the opportunity to hang out with friends. For more information, call 419-447-3751 or visit http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/.

item.title: Rubber Ducky Sensory Fun - 07/29/2026 11:00am EDT to 07/29/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Youth Services Department at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for "Rubber Ducky Sensory Fun" on Wednesday, July 29 at 11:00 a.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. This engaging event is designed for babies aged birth to 18 months and their caregivers, offering a variety of sensory experiences that support the babies' developing minds. The program is part of the 2026 Summer Reading Program theme “Unearth a Story.” For more information, visit the Junior Library, call 419-447-3751, or check the website at http://www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/.

item.title: "Shasta Daisies" Thursday, July 30, 2026 9am-12pm or 6pm-9pm #07302601 or #07302602 - 07/30/2026 9:00am EDT to 07/30/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by Friday, July 24. Paint "Shasta Daisies" in water mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on Thursday, July 30 from 9am until 12 pm or from 6pm-9pm. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 8"x16" canvas are included in the price of the class. Minimum class size 3 people, maximum of 8. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419 624 7020, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.title: 160 Years Celebration! An Evening of Faith and Fellowship - 07/31/2026 5:30pm EDT to 07/31/2026 6:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 7729 County Rd 29, Bellevue, OH 44811, USA Join us for an evening of fellowship as we celebrate more than a century of ministry, compassion, and service. Together, we'll reflect on our rich legacy of faith, care, and community; honor those who have helped shape our mission; and look forward to the opportunities and impact that lie ahead. Enjoy an evening of conversation while savoring a selection of heavy appetizers and desserts and explore the rich history of our organization through engaging displays and stories. We are honored to welcome Bishop Hee-Soo Jung, Resident Bishop of The Ohio Episcopal Area of The United Methodist Church, who will share an inspiring message. The evening will conclude with a silent auction, offering guests a meaningful opportunity to support our mission and help continue our legacy of service for generations to come.

item.title: Lantern Tour - 08/01/2026 6:00pm EDT to 08/01/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 15248 E Township Rd 178, Bellevue, OH 44811, USA Lantern Tour: Explore the Depths by Lantern Light! Step back in time and descend into the shadows on our captivating one-hour Lantern Tour! Relive the thrilling experience of early explorers as you venture 65 feet below the surface of Seneca Caverns. Guided by lantern light and your own headlamp, you’ll navigate rugged passageways and uncover the eerie ghost stories, local folklore, and fascinating history of this Ohio landmark. A Lantern Tour is the perfect way to step into adventure! What to Expect: A one-hour guided trek through the unique underground environment. Exploration by the atmospheric glow of lanterns and headlamps. Navigating natural stone steps and pathways, with some low-ceiling areas. Engaging tales of ghost stories, local legends, and the rich history of Seneca Caverns. Important Notes: Age Requirement: Suitable for ages 12 and older only due to the nature of the tour. What to Bring: Your own headlamp (optional, we will have some available if needed). What to Wear: A jacket or sweatshirt (cave temperature is 54°F) and closed-toe shoes (required). Booking: Advance registration is required. Ticket sales close 24 hours before the scheduled tour time. Group Size: Limited to 10 participants per tour. Spaces are limited, so book your adventure today! Price: $32 per person (ages 12 and older) Book Online Now
item.dc:creator: Seneca Caverns
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Lantern Tour on Aug 1, 2026 in Bellevue, OH. Featured themes include guided trek and lantern tour.

item.title: Grow With Me Planting Class - Kids Event - 08/02/2026 12:00pm EDT to 08/02/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Grow With Me Event Saturday May 30th, 2026 12:00pm Thanks to our amazing sponsor this event series will be admission free for the kids! Planting Words of Hope – Grow with Me Event Join us for a fun, creative, and meaningful hands‑on activity where kids will plant more than just seeds—they’ll plant hope! During this uplifting event, children will write their positive hopes, dreams, and encouraging words on special plant-able seed paper. Together, we’ll tuck those words into soil, symbolizing how positive thoughts can take root and grow over time. Kids will also decorate their own plant pots with affirmations and kind words about themselves, celebrating self‑confidence, creativity, and self‑love. As the seeds grow into plants, they’ll serve as a reminder that with patience, kindness, and care, great things can grow. This activity blends creativity, mindfulness, and nature in a joyful way that encourages kids to believe in themselves and their hopes for the future. Write it. Plant it. Watch it grow! Come create something beautiful today—and help it grow tomorrow. Ages 9 and up welcome! Follow this link to reserve your spot! https://www.zeffy.com/en-US/ticketing/grow-with-me-kids-event Space is limited so don't wait!
item.dc:creator: JAM's Monthly Markets
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Grow With Me Planting Class - Kids Event on Aug 2, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Kids & Family and Classes/Workshops.

item.title: 2026 Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing - 08/03/2026 8:00am EDT to 08/03/2026 3:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : 4399 OH-231, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

Mercy Health Foundation is hosting it's 35th Annual Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing, in support of Mercy Health - Tiffin & Willard Hospitals, will take place on Monday, August 3rd at Mohawk Golf & Country Club. It is a full day of fun and golf!

8:00 a.m - Registration/Driving Range Open

8:45 a.m. - Opening Announcements

9:00 a.m. - Tee Time

For sponsorship and recognition opportunities, please visit tinyurl.com/TiffinWillardGolf2026 to secure your spot!

For any questions about the event, please contact Jace Abel, Special Events Coordinator, at jabel@mercy.com or (567) 938-9429.

Dr. Walter A. Daniel was instrumental in establishing the Mercy Health Foundation — Tiffin in December of 1985. His outstanding dedication to rural health care will always be remembered. His example of philanthropy through leadership and generosity continues to thrive through the Mercy Health Foundation in the Tiffin and Willard communities and beyond. We honor his memory through the naming of this annual tournament that provides a time for fellowship as well as philanthropy.

Note of appreciation - The Leadership and associates of the Mercy Health — Tiffin and Willard Hospitals express sincere gratitude for your participation in the Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing. We treasure your continuous support of Mercy Health and are committed to providing excellent health care to the Tiffin and Willard communities.
item.dc:creator: Mercy Health Foundation
item.yodel:media_alt_text: 2026 Walter A. Daniel, MD Memorial Golf Outing on Aug 3, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include fundraising and golf.

item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Tiffin-Seneca Public Library’s virtual craft series for adults, “Make It At Home,” will offer free supply kits and an instructional video on how to decorate a candle with wax beginning Monday, August 3. Patrons can pick up a supply kit at the Information Desk full of almost everything needed to make a craft each month, and will only need to use a few basic supplies from home. An instructional video on how to decorate the candle will be posted on the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library YouTube channel and Facebook page. Please note that kits will be available on a first-come, first-served basis, and registration is not required. One kit per adult. Must be present to receive your kit. This program is free for adults. item.title: Electropneumatics - 08/04/2026 8:00am UTC to 08/04/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length: 16 Hours $1600 per student This hands-on two-day course introduces the fundamentals of pneumatic control systems and industrial automation. Participants will learn about control valves, pneumatic safety, print reading, and the operation of single- and double-acting cylinders. Through practical lab exercises, students will gain experience with directional control valves, pneumatic and electrical circuits, sensors, timers, regulators, and latching circuits. The course also emphasizes circuit analysis, troubleshooting, and repair skills through a series of real world application/tasks.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Electropneumatics on Aug 4, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include hands-on course and industrial.

item.title: History of Seneca County Agriculture - 08/04/2026 6:30pm EDT to 08/04/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 20 S Marion St, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Agriculture has always played a major role in Seneca County. But has it always looked like it does now? What were the major crops grown in Seneca County 150 years ago? What animals were raised in Seneca County in 1850? These and many more questions will be answered during this hour-long program. Learn about agriculture of Seneca County through the years and see exactly what that would have looked like. Registration is required by August 1 by contacting Bliss Memorial Library at (419) 983-4675.
item.dc:creator: Ohio State University Extension - Seneca County

item.title: Electropneumatics - 08/05/2026 8:00am UTC to 08/05/2026 4:30pm UTC
item.description: Location : 342 Perry St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Length: 16 Hours $1600 per student This hands-on two-day course introduces the fundamentals of pneumatic control systems and industrial automation. Participants will learn about control valves, pneumatic safety, print reading, and the operation of single- and double-acting cylinders. Through practical lab exercises, students will gain experience with directional control valves, pneumatic and electrical circuits, sensors, timers, regulators, and latching circuits. The course also emphasizes circuit analysis, troubleshooting, and repair skills through a series of real world application/tasks.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Learning Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Electropneumatics on Aug 5, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include learning center and pneumatic control systems.

item.title: Space Cats Summer Concert Series Presented by Downtown ReEvented - 08/07/2026 6:30pm EDT to 08/07/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 115 S Main St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA FREE SUMMER CONCERT DOWNTOWN! Local favorite The Space Cats are bringing their feel-good sound to Downtown Fostoria for a summer evening of live music, community, and fun in the alley! Downtown ReEvented is proud to present Space Cats as part of our Annual Summer Concert Series on Friday, August 7 from 6:30–8:30 PM in the alley between 113 and 115 S Main Street. This is a FREE community concert! Please note the updated time: 6:30–8:30 PM Bring your own chairs The road will be closed for the event Nearby food trucks + additional entertainment are in the works Space Cats always bring a great crowd, so plan to arrive early, grab your spot, and settle in for a fun summer night downtown. We’re working with other downtown businesses to coordinate nearby food trucks and entertainment to make it a full evening in Downtown Fostoria. More details will be shared as they’re confirmed. Come for the music, stay for the downtown fun!

item.title: Loud Fest 2026 - 08/07/2026 7:00pm EDT to 08/08/2026 11:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : Tiffin, OH 44883, USA

LOUD FEST OHIO IS BACK! AND IT'S BIGGER THAN EVER!

August 7–8, 2026 | Start Tiffin Fraternal Order of Eagles #402

Tiffin, Ohio — The ride that FIGHTS BACK.

Two days. Hundreds of bikes. One mission that matters more than all of it.

Every dollar raised goes directly to FACT — Financial Assistance for Cancer Treatment in Seneca County — putting real money in the hands of real people fighting the hardest battle of their lives. This isn't a party with a cause. This IS the cause. The party is just how we show up for it.

FRIDAY, AUGUST 7 — WARM UP NIGHT

Live Music 7PM–11PM | Band: Seventy80

Come early. Stay late. Get loud.

SATURDAY, AUGUST 8 — POKER RUN DAY

Registration: 10:30AM – Noon

Kickstands Up: Noon

Sound Logic: 1–4PM

Car Show: 1–4PM (All bikes & classic cars welcome)

Tim & Liz: 7–11PM

POKER RUN STOPS:

START/END — Tiffin Eagles 402

Stop 1 — Route 30 Harley-Davidson, Upper Sandusky

Stop 2 — Tavern 19

Stop 3 — Bellevue Eagles

Stop 4 — TBD

Stop 5 — Fostoria Eagles (pending)

REGISTRATION

Riders: $25 | Couples: $40

THANK YOU TO OUR 2026 SPONSORS:

Seneca Home Improvements • Steinmetz Signs and Graphic Ink • Arnold Vending • Waters Construction • Norwalk Concrete

You don't have to fight cancer alone in Seneca County — because WE ride for you.

Learn more about FACT: factsenecacounty.org
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Eagles #402
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Loud Fest 2026 on Aug 7, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Music & Entertainment and car show.

item.title: "Panda" Saturday, August 8, 2026 9am-12pm #08082601 - 08/08/2026 9:00am EDT to 08/08/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by Saturday August 1. Paint "Panda" in water mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on Saturday, August 8 from 9am until 12 pm. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 14"x18" canvas are included in the price of the class. Minimum class size 3 people, maximum of 8. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419 624 7020, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.title: Bloomville Block Party - 08/08/2026 9:00am EDT to 08/08/2026 11:00pm EDT
item.description:
Location : Bloomville, OH 44818, USA

The Bloomville Development Group is inviting families from across North Central Ohio to experience one of the area's largest free community celebrations when the annual Bloomville Block Party returns on Saturday, August 8.

The event transforms Bloomville into a day-long destination featuring something for every age, including a community parade, classic car show, live music, local food vendors, shopping, family activities, and community organizations.

One of the event's biggest highlights is its commitment to keeping family fun accessible. Thanks to the generosity of local sponsors, donors, and community fundraising efforts, children can enjoy attractions like the bounce houses and foam party completely free of charge.

Visitors can enjoy:

  • A community parade celebrating local organizations and businesses
  • A classic car show featuring vehicles from around the region
  • Cornhold Tournament
  • Live entertainment throughout the day
  • Food trucks and local vendors
  • Free children's activities, including bounce houses and a foam party
  • Community exhibits, raffles, and family-friendly entertainment

More than just a festival, the Bloomville Block Party showcases the strength of small-town collaboration. Dozens of volunteers, sponsors, businesses, civic organizations, and residents work together each year to create an event that welcomes both longtime community members and first-time visitors.
item.dc:creator: Bloomville Development Group
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Bloomville Block Party on Aug 8, 2026 in Bloomville, OH. Featured themes include Festivals/Fairs and bloomville.

item.title: Step Into the Arts in Downtown Fostoria! - 08/08/2026 1:00pm EDT to 08/08/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 214 S Main St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Step Into the Arts in Downtown Fostoria! Downtown ReEvented is excited to host a brand-new community arts event in Downtown Fostoria on Saturday, August 8, 2026 from 1:00–5:00 PM. Join us for an afternoon filled with creativity, live music, local artisans, food trucks, family fun, and community connection. This event will take place on Main Street between South Street and Tiffin Street, with Main Street closed for the event. Food trucks will be located in the city alley near the green space. Come enjoy: Live music Artisan market Food trucks Bounce house Face painting Silent auction Local handmade goods, artwork, crafts, woodwork, and homemade food items The Fostoria Farmers’ Market will also be happening earlier that morning, making it a perfect full day to spend downtown. Step Into the Arts is hosted by Downtown ReEvented, with net proceeds benefiting Tri-County Arts Initiative, a new Fostoria-based nonprofit serving Seneca, Hancock, and Wood counties. Tri-County Arts Initiative’s mission is to provide performing and creative arts opportunities for diverse audiences, underserved communities, and individuals with disabilities through inclusive programming in theater, dance, film, visual art, music, and sensory and tactile experiences. Bring the kids, invite your friends, support local artists, grab something to eat, and help us celebrate creativity in Downtown Fostoria. Let’s fill downtown with art, music, and community!
item.dc:creator: Downtown ReEvented
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Step Into the Arts in Downtown Fostoria! on Aug 8, 2026 in Fostoria, OH. Featured themes include crafts and family fun.

item.title: Samuel Harness LIVE - 08/08/2026 5:30pm EDT to 08/08/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : Charles Ash Bldg, 105 E Tiffin St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA We’re incredibly excited to announce that Samuel Harness will be performing LIVE at The UrbanWoody Brewery on Saturday, August 8th! If the name sounds familiar, Samuel gained national recognition on Season 21 of The Voice, where his soulful, gritty vocals earned him a spot on Team John Legend and made him a fan favorite across the country. Since then, he has continued to grow his fan base through powerful performances, original music, and a style that is uniquely his own. Whether you’ve followed his journey from television or you’re discovering him for the first time, you’re in for an unforgettable evening of live music right here in downtown Fostoria.

item.title: Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Meetings: August 2026 - 08/10/2026 7:00pm EDT to 08/10/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 96 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the next session of the Downtown Tiffin Town Hall Series: Connecting People, Ideas, and Progress on Monday, August 10, 2026, hosted by the Business Enhancement Committee at 7 PM. This session, titled “Empower Downtown: Tools, Resources & Collaboration for Success,” will focus on business development and support. Discover local, state, and national programs designed to strengthen downtown businesses—from workforce initiatives and job postings to grants, mentorship, training, and networking opportunities that foster collaboration and long-term success.

item.title: 150th Attica Independent Fair - 08/11/2026 9:00am EDT to 08/15/2026 11:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 15127 E Township Rd 12, Attica, OH 44807, USA The 150th Attica Independent Fair kicks-off Tuesday, August 11 at the fairgrounds in Attica, Ohio. The fair will feature livestock shows, grandstand entertainment, a beer garden, inflatables, kids' activities, food stands and more. The Attica Fair is one of only seven independent fairs remaining in the state of Ohio. As an independent fair, they have exhibitors from several counties and dedicated volunteers to make it all happen. Visit the website for more details! http://www.atticafair.com/ SCHEDULE (Subject to change) Sunday, August 9 9:00 am Booth display set-up and Barn decorating 11:00 am Antique Farm Machinery can be item.title: Mocktails (Wednesday August 12, 6:30-8) - 08/12/2026 6:30pm EDT to 08/12/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 89 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA If your sober-curious or alcohol-free hosting game needs an upgrade or if you are still serving your guests tap water, sugary soda, or a glass of plain cranberry juice, we need to have an intervention. You deserve better, your guests deserve better, and your morning-after energy definitely deserves better. Welcome to Herbal Mocktail Camp, a masterclass for non-drinkers, designated drivers, health-conscious folk, and anyone who wants to sip something sophisticated without the buzz. We are stripping away the boring wellness lectures and replacing them with plant magic, liquid alchemy, and a healthy dose of sass.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Community Kitchen
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Mocktails (Wednesday August 12, 6:30-8) on Aug 12, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include health conscious and mocktails.

item.title: "Lighthouse" Thursday, August 13, 2026 9am-12pm or 6pm-9pm #08132601 or #08132602 - 08/13/2026 9:00am EDT to 08/13/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Pre-registration and payment is required by Friday, August 7. Paint "Lighthouse" in water mixable oils with Carol Hoffman on Thursday, August 13 from 9am until 12 pm or from 6pm-9pm. Please arrive at least 15 minutes before class time. Carol will guide you through the process of creating this painting whether you are a beginner or more advanced painter. All of the materials including the 12"x16" canvas are included in the price of the class. Minimum class size 3 people, maximum of 8. Please bring a large box to take your painting home in. Cost is $50 for TAG members & $60 for non-members. Call Carol with questions. 419 624 7020, Tiffin Art Guild at 419-443-0478 (tiffinartguild@att.net), or stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday.

item.title: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance | Advocacy in Action: Engaging Local Leaders - 08/14/2026 11:00am EDT to 08/14/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Gillmor Student Center, 190 Miami St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development and United Way of North Central Ohio for the third Nonprofit Leadership Alliance quarterly meeting of 2026 on Friday, August 14 at 11 AM, hosted at Osceola Theatre at Tiffin University. This session is designed for nonprofit leaders, staff, and board members who want to better understand local decision-making and strengthen their ability to advocate for their organizations and the communities they serve. The featured topic, “Advocacy in Action: Engaging Local Leaders,” will bring together local and state leaders for a moderated discussion focused on public priorities, community challenges, and opportunities for collaboration with the nonprofit sector. Attendees will gain insight into how decisions are made and how nonprofits can effectively engage with elected officials and public leaders. The second half of the session will offer an interactive opportunity for nonprofit attendees to connect directly with the panelists—asking questions, sharing challenges, and practicing advocacy in a supportive, relationship-focused setting. These meetings are free and open to all nonprofit employees and board members in Seneca County. Chamber & Development membership is not required. Take advantage of this opportunity to build relationships, strengthen your advocacy skills, and engage directly with local leaders. Register now to secure your spot!
item.dc:creator: United Way of North Central Ohio
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Nonprofit Leadership Alliance | Advocacy in Action: Engaging Local Leaders on Aug 14, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include Business and advocacy skills.

item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The "End Human Trafficking" initiative focuses on raising awareness, providing education, and promoting action to combat human trafficking. Through dedicated efforts, it seeks to inform the public about the realities of trafficking, educate individuals on how to recognize and prevent it, and mobilize communities to take meaningful steps toward eradicating this grave issue.

item.title: Nevada Drop Off Recycling Weekend - 08/15/2026 12:00am EDT to 08/15/2026 11:59pm EDT
item.description: Location : 11385 Co Hwy 4, Carey, OH 43316, USA You can drop off your recyclables every month on the 3rd weekend of the month from Friday 4pm through Monday 8am here. We will take aluminum cans, steel cans, #1 and #2 plastics, any color of glass, newspaper, office paper, cardboard, chipboard and books. We ask that shredded paper be bagged. We also ask that your food and drink containers are rinsed clean. You can leave the caps on your items. https://www.co.wyandot.oh.us/calendar.aspx?EID=2161
item.dc:creator: Wyandot County Recycling Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: Nevada Drop Off Recycling Weekend on Aug 15, 2026 in Carey, OH. This is an all-day event with themes including Causes and Health & Fitness.

item.title: Car, Truck, Tractor Show - 08/15/2026 9:00am EDT to 08/15/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 290 S County Rd 7, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Bring any car, truck, or tractor you’d like to show off to our Car, Truck, and Tractor Show on August 15 at Hopewell Loudon School! Even if you don’t have anything to showcase, join us in admiring these vehicles and voting on your favorite ones in various categories! This is a fundraiser to help support the NRHL FFA Alumni Chapter. Our mission is to support the New Riegel and Hopewell Loudon FFA Chapters through fundraisers such as this one. Additionally, if you or your business would like to make a donation or are interested in being a sponsor for this event, email nrhlffaalumni@gmail.com Additional details: $10 entry fee per vehicle registered 9am-1pm

item.title: 19th Annual Summer Harvest Gourmet Gala - 08/15/2026 6:00pm EDT to 08/15/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 200 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Saturday, August 15 (6:00-9:00pm) 19th Annual Summer Harvest Gourmet Gala benefitting the Franciscan Earth Literacy Center & Seeds of Hope Farm will be held on the St. Francis Campus, 200 St. Francis Ave., Tiffin. Cost $50/person. Includes: Heavy appetizers made with chemical-free farm produce grown on the Seeds of Hope Farm provided by local restaurants and universities and assorted desserts, live cooking demonstrations and wine & brews from local wineries and breweries, and live music by Mimi Johnston. Ticket holders will also receive 1 general raffle entry, 1 Gift Card Tree (worth $250) entry and a Chance to Win $500 entry. Additional tickets may be purchased. There will also be a 50/50, Live/Silent and Online Auction Items, Wine Grab and more. Enter into as special "Florida Getaway" raffle donated by Tony and Robin Gorrell. This Waterfront House on the Isle of Palms Island, located in Treasure Island, Florida (north of St. Petersburg Beach) with a private pool and two full baths for up to 6 guests for one week (available September - December 2025 or April - December 15, 2026). Value: $2200. Tickets are $30 ea. or 4 for $100 and you do not need to be present to win. Don’t forget to bid on online auction items between Aug 7th – 14th at https://www.32auctions.com/FELCGala2026. Be entered into an Early Bird Drawing if ticket is purchased before July 1st https://dsnp.co/3JHZ7o. Winners of all raffle drawings will be announced at the event, posted on Facebook and contacted by phone. Deadline to purchase tickets: August 5th. For more information or for tickets, call Mission Advancement Office at 419-447-0435 or visit http://www.felctiffin.org/
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center
item.yodel:media_alt_text: 19th Annual Summer Harvest Gourmet Gala on Aug 15, 2026 in Tiffin, OH. Featured themes include franciscan earth literacy center and live music.
